title: Provider Runtime / Adapter SSOT

loop.observation: Provider runtime wrapper docs generate the C4 reader, but page fragments and sub-surfaces were still visible as loop-less manifest debt.
loop.hypothesis: The provider-runtime root can own the C4 wrapper evidence loop while focused sub-surfaces delegate or own narrower loops.
loop.execute: Generate the provider-runtime reader, verify compatibility markers, source anchors, and linked page fragments, and publish provider-runtime evidence in public CI.
loop.evaluate: The verifier fails when root markers, source checks, generated docs, or declared page fragments drift from the executable manifest.
loop.retrospective: C4 remains reviewable through a single generated wrapper while implementation-specific fragments stay small and delegated.

summary: This C4 entrypoint is generated from executable provider-runtime wrapper SSOT and delegates detailed ownership to focused provider runtime evidence tools.
The root remains a compatibility surface for existing CI anchors while the source of those anchors moves into this manifest.
compatibility_markers:
Provider full-access/trusted modes are not assumed from provider defaults or
caller arguments

Codex adapter 가 danger-full-access envelope 만 생성하고 그 위험을 Riido harness 가
관리한다

not a provider default, caller-provided default, or
  hidden fallback

Other providers should follow the same full-access/trusted-runtime
meta model only through provider-specific SSOT
C4 Provider Runtime / Adapter owns approval wait

source_checks.name: approval-timeout-owner
source_checks.contains: EventIngestor does not own timeout decisions

source_checks.name: draft-field-authority
source_checks.contains: EventIngestor remains the only append authority

assertions: C4 owns provider process/session/run lifecycle and adapter ACL outputs, not C3 capability, C5 scheduling, C6 workspace, C7 policy, C8 validation, or C2 canonical append authority.
Provider runtime wrapper docs are generated while detailed responsibility, ACL, gate, boundary, migration, draft field, and timeout evidence stays with dedicated tools.
Compatibility markers remain root-visible because existing public CI uses this root as a stable conformance surface.
